FluentGpu
Table of Contents

Class AcrylicBackdropMath

Namespace
FluentGpu.Render
Assembly
FluentGpu.Render.dll

Portable math for the in-app acrylic effect runner (design/subsystems/backdrop-effects-animation.md §2.3 two-pass PushLayer{Acrylic} schedule + §3 IEffectRunner seam, gpu-renderer.md §7 LayerPool). The GPU leaf (Rhi.D3D12 AcrylicCompositor) consumes these values so the region / size-bucket / kernel logic is headless-verifiable in the VerticalSlice while the COM/HLSL stays render-thread-confined in the leaf.

WinUI ground truth: microsoft-ui-xaml AcrylicBrush.h:64 sc_blurRadius = 30.0f, applied as Composition GaussianBlurEffect.BlurAmount (= the gaussian STANDARD DEVIATION, in DIPs) over the backdrop resolved onto the opaque FallbackColor (AcrylicBrush.cpp:500-528). The runner reproduces sigma = BlurSigma·dpiScale physical px with ONE fixed-sigma separable kernel by choosing the snapshot downsample factor instead of the kernel width: down = round(sigmaPhys / KernelSigma), so blurring the 1/down-resolution snapshot with the fixed sigma-7.5 kernel yields the requested full-resolution sigma (30 DIP ⇒ /4 at 100% DPI, /6 at 150%, /8 at 200%).

public static class AcrylicBackdropMath
Inheritance
object
AcrylicBackdropMath
Inherited Members
object.Equals(object)
object.Equals(object, object)
object.GetHashCode()
object.GetType()
object.MemberwiseClone()
object.ReferenceEquals(object, object)
object.ToString()

Fields

KernelRadius

Discrete kernel radius in downsampled texels (≈3σ support; the tail beyond carries <0.4% weight — the same truncation a D2D GaussianBlur uses for its kernel window).

public const int KernelRadius = 22

Field Value

int

KernelSigma

Gaussian standard deviation of the FIXED separable blur kernel, in downsampled texels.

public const float KernelSigma = 7.5

Field Value

float

TapCount

Bilinear-optimized taps per pass: the center texel + 11 linear taps, each folding a texel pair of the 45-texel discrete kernel into one bilinear fetch (offset weighted between the pair).

public const int TapCount = 12

Field Value

int

Properties

TapOffsets

Per-pass bilinear tap offsets in source texels (index 0 = center). One-sided; the shader mirrors.

public static ReadOnlySpan<float> TapOffsets { get; }

Property Value

ReadOnlySpan<float>

TapWeights

Per-pass tap weights (index 0 = center weight; indices 1.. are applied at ±offset, so the total kernel mass is w[0] + 2·Σ w[1..] == 1).

public static ReadOnlySpan<float> TapWeights { get; }

Property Value

ReadOnlySpan<float>

Methods

BucketDim(int)

LayerPool size bucket (gpu-renderer.md §7.1: "pooled RT textures keyed by quantized power-of-two-ish size buckets"): next power of two ≥ px, floor 64 — few distinct buckets ⇒ high cross-frame/cross-layer reuse, so steady state acquires from the free list and never creates a texture.

public static int BucketDim(int px)

Parameters

px int

Returns

int

DownsampleFactor(float, float)

Snapshot downsample divisor for a requested blur sigma (in DIPs — WinUI BlurAmount semantics) at a DPI scale. Effective full-resolution sigma = factor · KernelSigma ≈ blurSigmaDip · scale physical px (exact at 100/125/150/175/200% DPI for the WinUI sigma 30). Clamped to /8 — beyond that the bilinear down/up-sample artifacts outweigh kernel fidelity.

public static int DownsampleFactor(float blurSigmaDip, float scale)

Parameters

blurSigmaDip float
scale float

Returns

int

SnapshotRegion(in RectF, float, int, int, int, out int, out int, out int, out int)

The backdrop region to snapshot beneath a layer rect, in physical px clamped to the canvas: the rect inflated on every side by the full blur support (KernelRadius·down physical px), so every blurred texel under the rect samples REAL backdrop instead of a clamp-streaked edge (WinUI blurs the whole backdrop; inflating by the kernel support makes the region blur bit-identical inside the rect).

public static void SnapshotRegion(in RectF deviceRectDip, float scale, int down, int canvasW, int canvasH, out int x, out int y, out int w, out int h)

Parameters

deviceRectDip RectF
scale float
down int
canvasW int
canvasH int
x int
y int
w int
h int
FluentGpu — a near-zero-allocation, NativeAOT, GPU-rendered UI engine for .NET 10. Usage docs live here; architecture canon lives in design/ (SPEC-INDEX.md is the precedence authority).
